mission.title: Install and Configure llm-d
mission.description: llm-d is a high-performance distributed inference serving stack optimized for Kubernetes, designed to achieve state-of-the-art inference performance with modern accelerators. It is particularly useful for deploying large language models efficiently and reliably, with features such as intelligent inference scheduling and workload autoscaling. Key trade-offs include the complexity of setup and the need for adequate hardware resources.

steps.title: Install the CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the llm-d command-line interface, which is necessary for managing the deployment.

```bash

curl -LO https://github.com/llm-d/llm-d/releases/download/v0.5.1/llm-d-linux-amd64.tar.gz

tar -xzf llm-d-linux-amd64.tar.gz

sudo mv llm-d /usr/local/bin/

llm-d version

```

> Download the latest CLI binary.
> Extract the downloaded tarball.
> Move the CLI to a directory in your PATH.
> Verify the installation by checking the version.

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a new Kubernetes cluster using gcloud with the specified version and node count.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create llm-d-cluster --cluster-version=1.31 --num-nodes=3 --region=us-central1

```

> Create a cluster with 3 nodes in the specified region.

steps.title: Set Up Kubeconfig
steps.description:
This step configures the kubeconfig file to interact with the newly created cluster.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials llm-d-cluster --region=us-central1

```

> Fetch the cluster credentials.

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This step checks that the nodes in the cluster are up and running.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> List all nodes in the cluster to verify their status.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
This step sets up autoscaling and monitoring for the llm-d deployment.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/llm-d/llm-d/main/deploy/autoscaling.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/llm-d/llm-d/main/deploy/monitoring.yaml

```

> Apply the autoscaling configuration.
> Apply the monitoring configuration.

uninstall.title: Uninstall llm-d
uninstall.description:
This step removes the llm-d deployment from the cluster. Be aware that this will result in data loss and orphaned resources.

```bash

kubectl delete -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/llm-d/llm-d/main/deploy/deployment.yaml

gcloud container clusters delete llm-d-cluster --region=us-central1 --quiet

```

> Delete the llm-d deployment.
> Delete the entire cluster and its resources.

upgrade.title: Upgrade llm-d
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you backup your data. This process includes draining nodes, upgrading the deployment, and verifying the upgrade.

```bash

kubectl drain <node-name> --ignore-daemonsets

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/llm-d/llm-d/main/deploy/deployment.yaml

kubectl get pods -n llm-d

```

> Drain the node to prepare for upgrade.
> Upgrade the llm-d deployment.
> Verify that the pods are running after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to pull image'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified container image may not exist or is inaccessible.

**Fix:**
Check the image name and ensure it is available in the specified registry.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The cluster may not have enough resources to schedule the pods.

**Fix:**
Increase the number of nodes or the resources allocated to the existing nodes.

troubleshooting.title: Pods are in 'CrashLoopBackOff' state
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The application may be failing to start due to misconfiguration.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs using 'kubectl logs <pod-name>' for errors.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Connection refused'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The service may not be properly exposed or the pods are not ready.

**Fix:**
Ensure the service is correctly defined and check the pod status.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new hardware accelerators and improved inference scheduling algorithms.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the output of 'kubectl get pods' showing all pods in the 'Running' state with no errors.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the necessary tools and resources before proceeding with the installation.
